look_at.text: Computer Vision North America. I still can't decide if that's funny or just bureaucracy. Either way, I need their cost center.

A mounted plaque beside the door. 'Computer Vision North America — CVNA Lab' - use cost center to enter. North America. I'm fairly sure I'm in Germany. Nobody told North America we're in Germany. Or possibly they did and North America does not care. Either way — I need the cost center for this team.

use.text: It's a sign. I can only look at it.

look_at.text: A heavy door with a keypad. A small plaque reads: 'CVNA Lab — Authorized Access Only'. The keypad has 4 digit slots and a green confirm button.

open.text: The keypad blinks red. I need a 4-digit cost center code. I don't have it yet.

use.text: The lab door is already unlocked.
The keypad blinks red. I need a 4-digit cost center code. I don't have it yet.

push.text: It doesn't budge. The lock holds.

look_at.text: A standard office elevator with brushed steel doors. The floor panel is blank except for a sticky note: 'For HR, use 2-digit floor code.'

use.text: The floor panel is blank. I know the first digit — HR is on a floor starting with 2. I still need the second digit.
The floor panel is blank. I know the second digit — HR's floor ends in 8. I still need the first digit.
The floor panel is blank. I have no idea which floor HR is on.

open.text: The floor panel is blank. I know the first digit — HR is on a floor starting with 2. I still need the second digit.
The floor panel is blank. I know the second digit — HR's floor ends in 8. I still need the first digit.
The floor panel is blank. I have no idea which floor HR is on.

push.text: I press the call button. The elevator hums distantly and doesn't come.

look_at.text: A wall-mounted notice board. Outdated announcements: a pizza lunch from six months ago, a mandatory training reminder (overdue), and a dog-eared poster that reads 'INNOVATION: It starts with YOU.' Someone has added a question mark in blue pen.

use.text: The notice board doesn't need anything from me.

look_at.text: The package is already in my bag.
A small Amazon cardboard box sitting on the floor under the notice board. The label is addressed to someone whose desk I saw empty upstairs. Shaking it produces a muffled clunk.

pick_up.text: You pick up the package. It clunks once, significantly. You now have a new responsibility.

use.text: I should give this to whoever it's addressed to.

open.text: It's sealed shut with tape. I should give it to whoever it's addressed to.

look_at.text: The way back to the main office floor.

open.text: You head back to the office.

use.text: You head back to the office.
